rationale: Empirically probed panel.Create via a standalone scratch module (copied internal/panel source at pinned SHA 17a2ed28, built and run outside the bead worktree, worktree git status confirmed clean before/after) rather than trusting the shipped unit tests. 20/20 assertions passed: (1) first create writes panel.json+BRIEF.md header with round/sha and the stub body; (2) re-panel co-bumps round AND sha together in BOTH panel.json and the BRIEF header, never independently; (3) a pre-seeded skill-authored body (including a code fence) below the closing marker survives re-panel byte-for-byte; (4) a pre-seeded R1-round-1.json verdict file is left with identical bytes AND identical mtime after re-panel (proves it truly isn't rewritten, not just rewritten-with-same-content); (5) corrupt markers (open-only, and a harder case not in the shipped suite: duplicating an already-valid header+body pair after a real round-1 Create) both reject with an error and leave panel.json and BRIEF.md byte-identical and mtime-unchanged -- confirming the code path returns before either os.WriteFile call regardless of whether panel.json pre-existed.

findings.description: Adversarial bonus probe: if a skill-authored body (below the closing marker) ever quotes the literal marker strings verbatim (e.g. documenting the header syntax in a code fence), the NEXT re-panel Create fails with 'found 2 opening and 2 closing' because openCount/closeCount are counted across the whole file, not just the pre-splice header slot. This is a false-positive REJECTION, not a correctness bug: it fails safe (both files verified byte-identical and mtime-unchanged afterward, same as the true-corrupt case), matches G1's assigned lens on marker-corruption variants, and is an unlikely authoring pattern. Not blocking; worth a one-line doc note if Bead 5's skill ever has reason to quote the marker syntax.
